question_html: Would you expect benzaldehyde to be more reactive or less reactive in nucleophilic addition reactions than propanal? Explain your answer.
question_text: Would you expect benzaldehyde to be more reactive or less reactive in nucleophilic addition reactions than propanal? Explain your answer.
explanation: The carbon atom of the carbonyl group of benzaldehyde is less electrophilic than the carbon atom of the carbonyl group present in propanal. The polarity of the carbonyl group is reduced in benzaldehyde due to resonance making it less reactive in nucleophilic addition reaction as shown below and hence it is less reactive than propanal.

question_html:
Give reasons for the following:

  1. Benzoic acid does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction.
  2. {tex}{HCHO}{/tex} is more reactive than {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} towards addition of HCN.
  3. Vinyl group directly attached with carboxylic acid should decrease the acidity of corresponding carboxylic acid due to resonance, but on the contrary it increases the acidity.
question_text: Give reasons for the following: Benzoic acid does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction. {tex}{HCHO}{/tex} is more reactive than {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} towards addition of HCN. Vinyl group directly attached with carboxylic acid should decrease the acidity of corresponding carboxylic acid due to resonance, but on the contrary it increases the acidity.
explanation: Because the carboxyl group is deactivating and the catalyst aluminium chloride (Lewis acid) gets bonded to the carboxyl group.(forms salt) Because carbonyl carbon of HCHO is more electrophilic than {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} / due to +I effect of methyl group/ steric effect of methyl group, {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} is less reactive. Because of greater electronegativity of {tex}s p^{2}{/tex} hybridised carbon to which carboxyl carbon is attached.
solution: Because the carboxyl group is deactivating and the catalyst aluminium chloride (Lewis acid) gets bonded to the carboxyl group.(forms salt) Because carbonyl carbon of HCHO is more electrophilic than {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} / due to +I effect of methyl group/ steric effect of methyl group, {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} is less reactive. Because of greater electronegativity of {tex}s p^{2}{/tex} hybridised carbon to which carboxyl carbon is attached.
solution_html:
1. Because the carboxyl group is deactivating and the catalyst aluminium chloride (Lewis acid) gets bonded to the carboxyl group.(forms salt)
  2. Because carbonyl carbon of HCHO is more electrophilic than {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} / due to +I effect of methyl group/ steric effect of methyl group, {tex}{CH}_{3} {CHO}{/tex} is less reactive.
  3. Because of greater electronegativity of {tex}s p^{2}{/tex} hybridised carbon to which carboxyl carbon is attached.

Tollen's reagent acts as a mild oxidizing agent and oxidizes the aldehydes to corresponding carboxylic acids and itself gets reduced forming silver mirror. Thus, it shows positive test for aromatic aldehydes. OR {tex}CH_3CH_2\underset {\underset {OH}|}CHCH_3 \xrightarrow {\text {alkaline KMnO}_4}{/tex} CH 3 CH 2 COCH 3
solution_html:
1. This is called Etard reaction.
  2. This is because polarity of the carbonyl group is reduced in benzaldehyde due to resonance. Hence, it is less reactive towards nucleophilic addition reaction.
  3. Tollen's reagent acts as a mild oxidizing agent and oxidizes the aldehydes to corresponding carboxylic acids and itself gets reduced forming silver mirror. Thus, it shows positive test for aromatic aldehydes.

question_html:
Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow:
A class of organic molecules which contain a carbon atom connected to an oxygen atom by a double bond is called Aldehydes and Ketones. It is called as carbonyl group. Aldehydes are prepared by the oxidation of alcohols. Formaldehyde is sold in an aqueous solution called formalin. Propanone, a simplest ketone is commercially prepared by fermenting corn or by oxidation of propan-2-ol. Carboxylic acids also have carbonyl carbon. They can be prepared by the oxidation of alcohols and aldehydes.
Formic acid was first isolated by the distillation of red ants. It is partially responsible for the pain and irritation of ant and wasp stings. Aldehydes undergo many nucleophilic addition reactions. They can be reduced to primary alcohols. The aldehydes with -hydrogen undergo aldol condensation and the aldehydes without -hydrogen undergo Cannizzaro reaction.
Ketones are highly reactive, although less so than aldehydes. Ketones are obtained by oxidation of secondary alcohols. Ketones possessing {tex}\alpha{/tex}-hydrogens also undergo aldol condensation. Carboxylic acids occur widely in nature and are used in the production of plastics, esters, etc. Aspirin is prepared from acetic acid. Similar to aldehydes and ketones, carboxylic acids can be halogenated at the {tex}\alpha{/tex}-carbon by reacting with a halogen in presence of phosphorus.

  1. Which of the following compounds would undergo aldol condensation? (1)
    Methanal, Benzaldehyde, Ethanal
  2. Write the chemical test to distinguish between propanal and propanone. (1)
  3. Write the reagent required in the following reaction: (2)
    CH2 = CH - CH2OH {tex}\xrightarrow {?}{/tex} CH2 = CH - CHO
    OR
    Write the chemical reactions for Clemmensen reduction. (2)

Aldehydes and ketones are reduced to primary and secondary alcohols respectively by NaBH4 or LiAlH4 as well as catalytic hydrogenation. The carbonyl group of aldehydes and ketones is reduced to group on treatment with Zn-Hg and conc. HCl (Clemmensen reduction) or with hydrazine followed by NaOH or KOH in highly boiling solvent such as ethylene glycol (Wolff-Kishner reduction).
Aldehydes differ from ketones in their oxidation reactions. Aldehydes are easily oxidised to carboxylic acids on treatment with HNO3, KMnO4, K2Cr2O7 etc. Even mild oxidising agents mainly Tollens’ reagent and Fehling’s solution also oxidise aldehydes. Ketones are generally oxidised under vigorous conditions i.e., strong oxidising agents and at elevated temperatures, to give mixture of carboxylic acids having lesser number of C-atoms than the parent ketone.

  3. In the oxidation of by acidified K2Cr2O7, what is the products formed? (2)

    What is the appropriate reagent for the following transformation? (2)

question_html:
Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow:
The addition reaction of enol or enolate to the carbonyl functional group of aldehyde or ketone is known as aldol addition. The {tex}\beta{/tex}-hydroxyaldehyde or {tex}\beta{/tex}-hydroxyketone so obtained undergo dehydration in second step to produce a conjugated ketone. The first part of reaction is an addition reaction and the second part is an elimination reaction. Carbonyl compound having {tex}\alpha{/tex}-hydrogen undergoes aldol condensation reaction.

Mechanism :


  1. Condensation reaction is the reverse of which reaction? (1)
  2. What is the main product of an aldol condensation of acetaldehyde and acetone? (1)
  3. Which combination of carbonyl compounds gives phenyl vinyl ketone by an aldol condensation? (2)

    OR
    What is the main condition in aldol condensation? (2)
question_text: Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow: The addition reaction of enol or enolate to the carbonyl functional group of aldehyde or ketone is known as aldol addition. The {tex}\beta{/tex} -hydroxyaldehyde or {tex}\beta{/tex} -hydroxyketone so obtained undergo dehydration in second step to produce a conjugated ketone. The first part of reaction is an addition reaction and the second part is an elimination reaction. Carbonyl compound having {tex}\alpha{/tex} -hydrogen undergoes aldol condensation reaction. Mechanism : Condensation reaction is the reverse of which reaction? (1) What is the main product of an aldol condensation of acetaldehyde and acetone? (1) Which combination of carbonyl compounds gives phenyl vinyl ketone by an aldol condensation? (2) OR What is the main condition in aldol condensation? (2)
explanation: Condensation reaction is the reverse of hydrolysis, which splits a chemical entity into two parts through the action of the polar water molecule. CH 3 CH = CHCOCH 3 Acetophenone and Formaldehyde gives phenyl vinyl by an aldol condensation. OR In an aldol condensation, an enolate ion reacts with a carbonyl compound in the presence of acid/base catalyst to form a β-hydroxy aldehyde or β-hydroxy ketone, followed by dehydration to give a conjugated ketone
solution: Condensation reaction is the reverse of hydrolysis, which splits a chemical entity into two parts through the action of the polar water molecule. CH 3 CH = CHCOCH 3 Acetophenone and Formaldehyde gives phenyl vinyl by an aldol condensation. OR In an aldol condensation, an enolate ion reacts with a carbonyl compound in the presence of acid/base catalyst to form a β-hydroxy aldehyde or β-hydroxy ketone, followed by dehydration to give a conjugated ketone

1. Condensation reaction is the reverse of hydrolysis, which splits a chemical entity into two parts through the action of the polar water molecule.
  2. CH3CH = CHCOCH3
  3. Acetophenone and Formaldehyde gives phenyl vinyl by an aldol condensation.
    OR
